phrases.pinyin: děng le liǎng gè xiǎo shí
nán shēng bǐ nǚ shēng duō
phrases.english: There are more male students than female

key_sentences.pinyin: Tā bǐ nǐ dà sān suì.
key_sentences.english: He is three years older than you.

key_sentences.pinyin: Wǒ bǐ tā gāo yì diǎnr.
key_sentences.english: I am a little taller than her.

key_sentences.pinyin: Tā zǒu de bǐ wǒ kuài duō le.
key_sentences.english: He walks much faster than me.

key_sentences.chinese: 我等了两个小时，他才来。
key_sentences.pinyin: Wǒ děng le liǎng gè xiǎo shí, tā cái lái.
key_sentences.english: I waited for two hours before he came.

key_sentences.pinyin: Tā lái de bǐ tā zǎo.
key_sentences.english: She arrived earlier than he did.

key_sentences.pinyin: Nán shēng bǐ nǚ shēng pǎo de kuài.
key_sentences.english: Male students run faster than female students.

grammar_points.title: 「比」字句(2) — The bǐ Sentence Summary 2
grammar_points.explanation: The 比 sentence is used to make comparisons. Structure: A + 比 + B + Adjective (+ Degree/Amount). To express a specific difference: A + 比 + B + Adjective + Amount (e.g., 他比你大三岁). Negative: A + 没有 + B + Adjective (A is not as … as B). 比 cannot be used with 很/非常. A modifier of degree (多了/一点儿) can follow.

examples.pinyin: Tā bǐ nǐ dà sān suì.
examples.english: He is three years older than you.

examples.pinyin: Zhè tiáo lù bǐ nà tiáo cháng duō le.
examples.english: This road is much longer than that one.

exercises.question: How do you say 'She is 5 cm taller than him'?
exercises.correct_pinyin: Tā bǐ tā gāo wǔ lí mǐ.

exercises.question: What does 没有 mean in comparison sentences?
exercises.correct: Is not as ... as (A is less than B)
options: Is not as ... as (A is less than B)

exercises.question: Fill in: 今天___昨天冷多了。(Today is much colder than yesterday.)
What does 比你大三岁 mean?
option_pinyin: bǐ nǐ gāo sān lí mǐ

grammar_points.title: 时量补语 — Time-Measure Complement
grammar_points.explanation: The time-measure complement expresses the duration of an action. Structure: Verb + 了 + Duration. If there is an object, it usually goes between the verb and the duration, or the verb is repeated: Verb + Object + Verb + 了 + Duration. Examples: 我等了两个小时 (I waited for two hours); 她学了三年中文 (She studied Chinese for three years).

examples.pinyin: Wǒ děng le liǎng gè xiǎo shí.
Tā xué le sān nián zhōng wén.
examples.english: She studied Chinese for three years.

exercises.question: What does the time-measure complement express?
options: How many times an action happened

exercises.question: Which sentence correctly uses a time-measure complement?
exercises.correct_pinyin: Tā pǎo le sān shí fēn zhōng.

exercises.question: How do you say 'I slept for eight hours'?
exercises.correct_pinyin: Wǒ shuì le bā gè xiǎo shí.

exercises.question: What does 等了两个小时 mean?
option_pinyin: děng le liǎng gè xiǎo shí

grammar_points.title: 动量补语 — Action-Measure Complement
grammar_points.explanation: The action-measure complement expresses how many times an action occurred. Structure: Verb + 了 + Number + 次/遍/回 (+ Object). 次 (cì) and 遍 (biàn) are the most common measure words for actions. 次 = times (a count); 遍 = times (from start to finish). Examples: 我去了两次 (I went twice); 她读了三遍 (She read it three times through).

examples.pinyin: Wǒ qù Běijīng qù le liǎng cì.
Tā shuō le sān biàn, wǒ cái dǒng.
examples.english: He said it three times before I understood.

exercises.question: What does the action-measure complement express?
exercises.correct: How many times an action occurred
options: How many times an action occurred

exercises.question: Which sentence correctly uses an action-measure complement?
exercises.correct_pinyin: Tā dǎ le tā sān cì diàn huà.

exercises.question: What is the difference between 次 and 遍?
exercises.correct: 次 counts occurrences; 遍 means from beginning to end each time
options: 次 counts occurrences; 遍 means from beginning to end each time
次 is for people; 遍 is for things
次 is formal; 遍 is casual

exercises.question: What does 比以前高了 mean?

quiz.question: Fill in: 他___我___三岁，他___得___我快多了。(He is three years older than me and runs much faster than me.)
quiz.correct: 比 / 大 / 跑 / 比
options: 比 / 大 / 跑 / 比
大 / 比 / 比 / 跑
比 / 高 / 走 / 比
多 / 快 / 比 / 早

quiz.question: What does 比她高一点儿 mean?
What does 走得比我快 mean?
How do you say 'arrived earlier than him'?

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 小明，你比你哥哥大还是小？
lines.pinyin: Xiǎo Míng, nǐ bǐ nǐ gē ge dà hái shì xiǎo?
lines.english: Xiao Ming, are you older or younger than your brother?

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 他比我大三岁，他二十五岁了。
lines.pinyin: Tā bǐ wǒ dà sān suì, tā èr shí wǔ suì le.
lines.english: He is three years older than me; he is twenty-five now.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 比我高一点儿，他跑步也比我快多了。
lines.pinyin: Bǐ wǒ gāo yì diǎnr, tā pǎo bù yě bǐ wǒ kuài duō le.
lines.english: A little taller; he also runs much faster than me.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 你哥哥很厉害！他每天跑多长时间？
lines.pinyin: Nǐ gē ge hěn lì hai! Tā měi tiān pǎo duō cháng shí jiān?
lines.english: Your brother is impressive! How long does he run every day?

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 他每天跑了一个多小时，我只跑了三十分钟。
lines.pinyin: Tā měi tiān pǎo le yí gè duō xiǎo shí, wǒ zhǐ pǎo le sān shí fēn zhōng.
lines.english: He runs for over an hour every day; I only run for thirty minutes.

lines.speaker: A
lines.pinyin: Nǐ jīn tiān bǐ zuó tiān lái de zǎo!
lines.english: You came earlier today than yesterday!

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 是的，我早了三十分钟，因为比赛很重要。
lines.pinyin: Shì de, wǒ zǎo le sān shí fēn zhōng, yīn wèi bǐ sài hěn zhòng yào.
lines.english: Yes, I came thirty minutes earlier because the competition is important.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 今天你跑得比上次快多了！
lines.pinyin: Jīn tiān nǐ pǎo de bǐ shàng cì kuài duō le!
lines.english: Today you ran much faster than last time!

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 谢谢，我练习了两次，每次一个小时。
lines.pinyin: Xiè xie, wǒ liàn xí le liǎng cì, měi cì yí gè xiǎo shí.
lines.english: Thank you; I practised twice, one hour each time.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 很好，男生女生都要多练习！
lines.pinyin: Hěn hǎo, nán shēng nǚ shēng dōu yào duō liàn xí!
lines.english: Very good; both male and female students should practise more!
